description:
Goal
Resolve the current dirty convergence work into intentional ticket commits before the dynamic activation-cycle work starts. Keep only foundational changes that are independently justified by tests and telemetry: structural activation partition classes, `ActivationInputs` as body evidence, list-cons typing in the type kernel, callable identity preservation when it is semantically necessary, exact artifact-rung demands that are already stable, and the native CPS back-edge fix only if it lands under `fz-hn8` first.

Signal
- The native JIT tail-recursion edit is split and closed through `fz-hn8`, or absent from this commit.
- The worktree becomes clean through a coherent foundation commit; unrelated edits are removed, ticketed, or split, not smuggled into the convergence ticket.
- Focused tests cover each retained foundation fact: callable identity preservation under widening, list cons non-empty construction, activation key/body evidence split, activation-input contributor replacement, stable artifact-rung demands, and the quicksort finite-frontier budget.
- The attempted `SealSemanticClosure` settled-read cleanup is not in this commit; its re-demand churn is captured by `fz-rh2.18.9.10`.
- Real quicksort telemetry runs under `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout`, resolves within the activation-partition budget, and does not produce runaway logs.
- If the broad `cargo test --lib compiler2::` remains blocked by later dynamic-cycle/seal/Enum work, those blockers are carried by `fz-rh2.18.9.7` through `fz-rh2.18.9.10`; no untracked known failure is left behind.
- No debug prints, temporary fixtures, fixture-name special cases, qsort-specific logic, Enum.reduce-specific logic, or unchecked `fz2` run remains.

Strategy
- Treat every modified file as suspect until re-derived from an observed failure and covered by a focused test.
- Split ticket ownership before commit: native back-edge work belongs to `fz-hn8`; semantic partition foundation belongs here; dynamic cycle convergence and honest semantic sealing belong to the follow-on tickets.
- Keep activation identity and body input evidence separate everywhere. `ActivationKey` is the partition identity; `ActivationInputs` is the joined caller evidence consumed by analysis.
- Move source-shape facts such as `[head | tail]` into the type kernel so semantic lowering does not carry local approximations.
- Preserve callable identity only where call discovery requires it; otherwise let partition policy and `ActivationInputs` carry evidence.
- Run dangerous `fz2 --log-telemetry` probes only with `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout` and compiler drive timeouts.

Outro
No mixed-ticket native edit, no qsort/Enum/runtime-helper special cases, no local semantic list workaround, no closure identity erasure that loses callable targets, no broken `SealSemanticClosure` settled-read cleanup, no pass-shaped artifact test assumption that hangs under re-demand, no hidden native stack-overflow regression, no unbounded telemetry command, and no claim that static `Recursive(function)` solves dynamic activation cycles.

comments.text: Audit note: the attempted jobs/root.rs settled-read cleanup was removed from the .9.5 dirty batch because it made quicksort backend/materialization re-demand tests time out. Captured as fz-rh2.18.9.10 and wired after dynamic cycle/stored-state publication.

title: Compiler2 convergence: land activation partition foundation and split dirty state
